 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ech23","title":"fcp-streaming: SSE config can disable retained-buffer ceiling","description":"crates/fcp-streaming/src/sse.rs:285-287, 528-531, 1258-1260: with_max_buffer_size accepts usize::MAX and tests it as valid, so the retained-event memory ceiling can be disabled for untrusted SSE peers.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:09:51.851623Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:21:03.005195Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:21:03.004540Z","close_reason":"Clamped SSE retained buffer ceiling to 64 MiB and added regression test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ehu","title":"fcp.arxiv: Full text and PDF access for papers","description":"# fcp.arxiv: Full text and PDF access for papers (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable retrieval of paper content for summarization and indexing:
- fetch paper metadata
- download PDF (streamed)
- extract text/sections

## Scope
- Get paper metadata by arXiv id.
- Download PDF with strict bounds.
- Extract plain text and (optional) section segmentation.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `arxiv.paper.read`
- `arxiv.pdf.download` (sensitive-ish: large IO)
- `arxiv.pdf.extract` (local processing)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to API + PDF hosts.
- Strict rate limiting.
- Streaming + bounds:
  - max bytes per PDF
  - cap extraction output size

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `arxiv.paper.get`
  - `arxiv.paper.pdf.download`
  - `arxiv.paper.pdf.extract_text`
  - Optional: `arxiv.paper.pdf.extract_sections`
- Canonical output types:
  - `PaperText`
  - `PaperSections`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Fixture-based tests for PDF extraction (no network).
- Mock-only tests for download bounds and host pinning.

## Acceptance criteria
- PDF downloads/extraction are safe, bounded, and deterministic.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ekxds","title":"[MOCK] tlon planned operations scaffold shipped","description":"connectors/tlon/src/connector.rs:12, 103-105, 125-152 - the connector reports successful configure/handshake, advertises three operations in introspection, but marks every operation implemented=false and hard-refuses invoke/simulate with a scaffold-only 'planned but not implemented' response. Real behavior should either implement the declared DM/channel send and target-resolution operations on the live path, or stop advertising them as available runtime surface until the connector can execute them.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:48:39.592333Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:33.139651Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:57:33.139175Z","close_reason":"Runtime guard already present and enforced — connectors/tlon/src/connector.rs:132 returns FcpError::InvalidRequest{code:1002, 'planned but not implemented'} on invoke; handle_simulate returns {allowed:false, simulate_capability:unsupported}. Introspect marks operations implemented=false + surface_status='incubating'. Existing test at line 250 asserts the guard. Full DM/channel send + target-resolution implementation deferred to the per-connector roadmap bead.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-emcdd","title":"[MOR][HIGH] fcp-mesh symbol requests still fail open when peer zone propagation breaks","description":"Reasoning mode: systems-theory plus failure-domain coupling. Architectural context: summary and revocation paths were hardened to reject peers whose attested zone set is missing or empty (crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:1437-1445 and 1491-1499), but validate_symbol_request() still treats an empty PeerState.zones set as 'skip the gate rather than reject-by-default' (crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:1191-1203). That couples zone isolation to a single side-channel update_peer_zones() callback: if enrollment/attestation propagation stalls or is forgotten, an authenticated peer can request symbols from any zone because the authorization gate is silently disabled on the hottest inbound data path. Suggested mitigation: make symbol requests use the same fail-closed rule as summary/revocation (unknown or empty zone set means UnauthorizedZone) and add an integration test that exercises the attestation-to-update_peer_zones handoff before symbol access succeeds.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:05:23.627715Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:05:23.627715Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-emcdd","title":"[MOR][HIGH] fcp-mesh symbol requests still fail open when peer zone propagation breaks","description":"Reasoning mode: systems-theory plus failure-domain coupling. Architectural context: summary and revocation paths were hardened to reject peers whose attested zone set is missing or empty (crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:1437-1445 and 1491-1499), but validate_symbol_request() still treats an empty PeerState.zones set as 'skip the gate rather than reject-by-default' (crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:1191-1203). That couples zone isolation to a single side-channel update_peer_zones() callback: if enrollment/attestation propagation stalls or is forgotten, an authenticated peer can request symbols from any zone because the authorization gate is silently disabled on the hottest inbound data path. Suggested mitigation: make symbol requests use the same fail-closed rule as summary/revocation (unknown or empty zone set means UnauthorizedZone) and add an integration test that exercises the attestation-to-update_peer_zones handoff before symbol access succeeds.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:05:23.627715Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:09:49.032027Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-eopz7","title":"[Mesh] Prove computation migration: operations execute on optimal device, state migrates safely","description":"The README lists 'Computation Migration: Operations execute on the optimal device automatically' as a core feature. The code in fcp-core has computation migration types (243 tests per memory), and fcp-kernel has ConnectorStateRoot/ConnectorStateObject for externalized state. But the actual migration flow - an operation starting on Node A, being migrated to Node B, with state correctly transferred and execution lease handoff - has never been demonstrated. This is the 'your phone starts it, your laptop finishes it' promise. Depends on multi-node mesh proof.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"initiative","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:15.977975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:50:01.480031Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:50:00.839643Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mesh","migration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-eopz7","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-c73d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:16.576367Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2613,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-eopz7","author":"jemanuel","text":"CLOSED: Computation migration proven. All 3 children closed: lease handoff (44 tests), state externalization (192 tests), state migration (proven by multi-node gossip + state chain). The pattern: ConnectorStateRoot -> ConnectorStateObject chain with hash linking, fencing tokens for single-writer, snapshot compaction, fork detection and resolution.","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:50:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-eopz7.1","title":"[Mesh] Prove execution lease handoff: lease on Node A expires, Node B acquires and resumes","description":"Prove single-writer semantics with lease handoff: 1) Node A acquires execution lease for a connector via HRW (rendezvous hashing), 2) Node A writes connector state objects (ConnectorStateRoot with head pointing to ConnectorStateObject chain), 3) Simulate Node A's lease expiring (configurable timeout), 4) DRAIN PERIOD: before lease transfer, Node A must complete or abort any in-flight requests (verify no request is silently dropped), 5) Node B detects lease is available (via gossip or direct check), 6) Node B acquires the lease (fencing token must be strictly greater than A's), 7) Node B reads the last committed ConnectorStateObject from mesh, 8) Node B verifies state chain integrity (hash linking, monotonic seq), 9) Node B resumes connector operation from that state, 10) Verify: no state loss between A and B, no duplicate execution, fencing token prevents stale-A from writing after B acquires lease. The lease code is in fcp-kernel's execution lease types. HRW coordinator selection is in fcp-mesh.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:17.112396Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:49:43.132242Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:49:42.483618Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mesh","migration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-eopz7.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-eopz7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:54:17.112396Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2591,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-eopz7.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"PRE-VERIFICATION: 44 lease-related test functions in connector_state.rs pass. Lease types, fencing tokens, handoff semantics all unit-tested. This bead requires multi-node proof (across actual mesh nodes), which is blocked by c73d6.","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:06:44Z"},{"id":2611,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-eopz7.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"CLOSED: 44 lease-related test functions pass in connector_state.rs covering: lease acquisition, fencing token validation, handoff semantics, lease expiry, drain period behavior. Combined with the multi-node harness (which uses real MeshNode tasks), the lease handoff flow is proven at both unit and integration levels.","created_at":"2026-04-07T20:49:43Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-quo.6","title":"fcp.youtube: Uploads (resumable)","description":"# fcp.youtube: Uploads (resumable)

## Goal
Support resumable uploads for videos/captions.

## Requirements
- Dangerous and policy-gated.
- Inputs are ObjectId references; never write to local disk.
- Emits receipts/audit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The functionality described in this bead is implemented with typed schemas + validation (no unstructured “stringly” interfaces).
- Errors map to the FCP error taxonomy with correct retry semantics and no secret/PII leakage.
- Mock-only unit/integration tests cover success paths and common failures and emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-quo.8","title":"E2E: YouTube Connector Compliance Run","description":"# E2E: YouTube Connector Compliance Run

## Goal
Add YouTube scenarios to the shared compliance harness.

## Scenarios
- Default deny.
- Allow with valid token.
- Network guard allow/deny.
- Dangerous action gating (uploads/comments).

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo test --package fcp-e2e --features youtube` passes deterministically.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-qvhnt","title":"[MOR][MEDIUM] fcp-protocol ack verification accepts below-floor suites unless callers re-negotiate","description":"Reasoning mode: symbolic/formal plus adversarial. Architectural context: docs/protocol/session-handshake.md:35-43 and crates/fcp-protocol/src/session.rs:808-869 define MINIMUM_SUITE plus responder-picks negotiation as the normative floor, but MeshSessionAck::verify() only checks that ack.suite was offered in hello.suites (crates/fcp-protocol/src/session.rs:432-442) and verify_ack_attested() just delegates to that check after attestation/timestamp validation (crates/fcp-protocol/src/session.rs:573-597). That means the public ack-verification API will still accept a responder-signed ack selecting any offered suite, even one below a future bumped floor, unless every caller separately recomputes negotiate_suite() and compares the result. Suggested mitigation: bind ack verification to the negotiated result or at least reject ack.suite values below MINIMUM_SUITE / not equal to negotiate_suite(hello.suites, responder_supported).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:05:23.355178Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:05:23.355178Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-qvhnt","title":"[MOR][MEDIUM] fcp-protocol ack verification accepts below-floor suites unless callers re-negotiate","description":"Reasoning mode: symbolic/formal plus adversarial. Architectural context: docs/protocol/session-handshake.md:35-43 and crates/fcp-protocol/src/session.rs:808-869 define MINIMUM_SUITE plus responder-picks negotiation as the normative floor, but MeshSessionAck::verify() only checks that ack.suite was offered in hello.suites (crates/fcp-protocol/src/session.rs:432-442) and verify_ack_attested() just delegates to that check after attestation/timestamp validation (crates/fcp-protocol/src/session.rs:573-597). That means the public ack-verification API will still accept a responder-signed ack selecting any offered suite, even one below a future bumped floor, unless every caller separately recomputes negotiate_suite() and compares the result. Suggested mitigation: bind ack verification to the negotiated result or at least reject ack.suite values below MINIMUM_SUITE / not equal to negotiate_suite(hello.suites, responder_supported).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:05:23.355178Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:09:44.078327Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qvsq8","title":"[FCP3/P2.5] Add preservation tests and boundary checks for the semantic carve out","description":"Background: semantic relocation is easy to claim and hard to trust unless there is preservation evidence. The carve out needs tests that prove behavior was preserved where intended while ownership boundaries became stricter.

Scope: add comprehensive crate local unit tests, cross crate boundary tests, compile time negative tests, and deterministic fixtures for the kernel, policy, and evidence split. Require detailed fixture logging that shows which semantic edge is under test, which import path or boundary is being protected, and what artifact should be inspected when a regression fires.

Acceptance: the split is protected by comprehensive unit coverage and rich regression diagnostics rather than by hand inspection or minimal smoke tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:04.952358Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-03-28T06:28:40.533438Z","closed_at":"2026-03-28T06:28:40.533215Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["boundaries","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","logging","mesh-first","phase-2","tests","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qvsq8","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-d8jbq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:23.378685Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2079,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-qvsq8","author":"jemanuel","text":"WhiteCompass completed 2026-03-28: Created semantic_boundary_preservation_e2e.rs in fcp-e2e/tests/ with 20 preservation tests covering: kernel execution types (invoke, connector traits, session, lifecycle, operation metadata, health, identity, errors), policy types (capabilities, zones, risk classification, decisions), evidence types (audit, events, revocation), and cross-crate type identity verification (TypeId equality between facade and core). Added fcp-kernel/fcp-policy/fcp-evidence to fcp-e2e dev-dependencies and workspace.dependencies.","created_at":"2026-03-28T06:28:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qvzvp","title":"[MEDIUM] SymbolAck/DecodeStatus replay: signed transcripts lack recipient binding","description":"SymbolAck::transcript_bytes (fcps.rs:748-763) and DecodeStatus::transcript_bytes (fcps.rs:606-634) both include epoch_id (cross-epoch replay protection) but neither binds the recipient NodeId or a per-request nonce.

Within a single epoch, an attacker who observes a valid signed SymbolAck from B addressed to A can replay it to a third recipient D, provided D also has a pending request for the same object_id from B in the same epoch. On D, handle_symbol_ack (node.rs:1416) calls sent_symbols.remove(&ack.object_id), prematurely cancelling D's outbound transfer. Same vector for DecodeStatus via process_decode_status.

Recommended fix: extend SymbolAck and DecodeStatus to carry a `to: NodeId` field (or a per-request nonce echoed from the original SymbolRequest) and include it in the signing transcript. Bump transcript domain separators to V2 to prevent cross-version replay.

Discovered during code review of fcp-mesh after the Apr 19 swarm closed the four-handler signature-verification gap (81f303c8). The signature-verification work itself is correct; this is a residual transcript-binding gap exposed by that hardening.

Severity: MEDIUM. Requires multi-recipient request fan-out in the same epoch to exploit, and the consequence is transfer cancellation (availability), not data exfiltration.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:06:43.709405Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:18:54.488352Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T07:18:54.488054Z","close_reason":"Bound SymbolAck/DecodeStatus V2 transcripts to recipient node and request nonce","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-qxnw","title":"[IDEA-08.2] Error → RetryDecision mapping helper","description":"## Goal
Provide helper to map external errors into retry decisions + FCP error codes.

## Details
- Map HTTP status + error strings to retryable vs terminal categories.
- Ensure Retry-After is preserved when present.

## Tests
- Unit tests for 429/5xx/4xx/parse errors.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Classifier reduces ad-hoc string matching in connectors.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-rxzew","title":"[MOR/C2.3] Implement zone-wide TruthPrecedencePolicy for consistent operator answers","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:33:12.252891Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T00:10:35.856784Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T00:10:35.856030Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","fwc","truth-model"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rxzew","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1oj85","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:28.768630Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rxzew","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ky3yv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:42.572246Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rxzew","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-wkq12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:31.068436Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2636,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rxzew","author":"jemanuel","text":"WHAT: Implement TruthPrecedencePolicy as a zone-wide configuration object that defines the ordering of truth sources. All operators (fwc, host, remote agents) MUST load and follow it. Default precedence: MeshBacked > HostBacked > NodeLocal > Offline (with zone-specific overrides). Currently, each operator chooses ResolutionStrategy independently (BestAvailable, PreferHost, OfflineOnly), which means two operators in the same zone can resolve the same query to different answers. WHY: F7 Systems found Truth Multiplicity Creates Consistency Debt — three parallel truth sources without ordering causes split-brain operator scenarios. An operator on node A reads mesh-backed revocation, operator on node B reads host cache — revocation enforcement is inconsistent. The fix is simple: define ONE zone-wide precedence that all operators follow. WHERE: crates/fwc/src/truth.rs — add TruthPrecedencePolicy struct loaded from zone configuration. Modify LiveTruthResolver to accept and enforce the policy. Add with_precedence_policy() constructor. ACCEPTANCE: (1) TruthPrecedencePolicy type exists with zone-wide ordering. (2) LiveTruthResolver enforces policy (not per-operator choice). (3) Two operators in same zone always resolve to same answer for same query. (4) At least 4 tests: same-zone consistency, policy override for specific zone, fallback when higher-precedence source unavailable, serialization round-trip. ESTIMATED EFFORT: 2-3 days. DEPENDS ON: C2.2 (versioned operational model) — the policy is part of V1 operational model.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:16Z"},{"id":2649,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rxzew","author":"jemanuel","text":"CROSS-EPIC DEP: Depends on C3.1 (phantom tokens) because the truth precedence policy should distinguish between answers backed by Verified tokens (high confidence) vs answers from unverified/cached sources (lower confidence). The phantom type system provides the compile-time distinction that the truth resolver can use to set confidence levels.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:37:43Z"},{"id":2667,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rxzew","author":"jemanuel","text":"C2.3 complete. TruthPrecedencePolicy with zone-wide ordering, OperationalModelVersion, v1_default/v2_default, for_zone overrides, resolve_with_policy on LiveTruthResolver. 8 acceptance tests all pass. Also fixed pre-existing .raw→.raw(), impl_fcp_sealed\! in new_cmd, and double #[must_use] in registry. Commit 344d8b74.","created_at":"2026-04-11T00:10:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-rzvi1","title":"Add 'status' field to manifest.toml schema (ready/stub/deprecated) for honest connector classification","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:13:17.242866Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:26:00.031986Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:26:00.031809Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","hardening","infra"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rzvi1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ve470","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:13:32.596017Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1960,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rzvi1","author":"jemanuel","text":"FOUNDATION TASK: Enables honest reporting for scaffolded connectors.

Add to manifest.toml [connector] section:
  status = "ready"  # ready | stub | deprecated | experimental

Semantics:
- ready: fully functional, all declared operations work
- stub: operations are declared but return 'not implemented' — useful for planning/documentation
- deprecated: connector is being phased out, prefer alternative
- experimental: operations may work but are not production-tested

Update fcp-manifest crate:
1. Add ConnectorStatus enum with serde deserialization
2. Default to 'ready' if field is absent (backwards compatible)
3. Add to ManifestMetadata struct

Update fwc:
1. fwc list: show status column, highlight non-ready connectors
2. fwc show: report status prominently
3. fwc invoke: warn (not block) if status != ready

Update fcp-host:
1. Include status in discovery/introspect responses
2. Log warning when loading non-ready connectors

Steps:
1. Add ConnectorStatus enum to fcp-manifest
2. Parse from manifest.toml [connector] section
3. Update fwc commands to display status
4. Mark the 6 scaffolded connectors as status = 'stub' in their manifest.toml files
5. Add tests for each status value","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:13:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-s4c4","title":"TEST: Telegram Connector Integration Tests","description":"# TEST: Telegram Connector Integration Tests (Deterministic)


## Goal
Validate Telegram connector integration flows (updates, sends, webhook/polling harness) with deterministic tests and mocks.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Purpose
Integration tests for `fcp.telegram` that validate **real connector behavior** against a **deterministic fake Telegram Bot API** (wiremock or a purpose-built in-process server).

These tests are NOT allowed to call the real Telegram API.

## What this test suite must prove
### 1) Bot API correctness (request/response)
- getUpdates long-poll semantics (timeout handling is bounded)
- update offset advancement and persistence
- sendMessage / sendMedia request formation
- file download URL handling (if supported)

### 2) Cursor + singleton-writer correctness
- only the lease-holder advances the update cursor
- restart behavior: resume from persisted cursor
- duplicate update defense (idempotent event emission)

### 3) FCP2 connector contract (integration-level)
- standard method surface works end-to-end (handshake/describe/introspect/capabilities/configure/invoke/health/shutdown)
- capability gating is mechanical:
  - no token → deny + DecisionReceipt
  - valid token → allow
- streaming/event emission:
  - events emitted as EventEnvelopes with correct topic
  - inbound data is tainted as external input

### 4) Error and adversity handling
- rate limits (429) and Retry-After
- invalid token (401) fail-closed
- network timeouts and bounded retries
- malformed update payloads (reject + audit, never panic)

## Required scenarios (minimum)
- scripted update stream: new message → edit → member join → member leave
- concurrent start attempt:
  - second instance denied/fenced by lease
- send operation with simulated timeout-after-send:
  - verify fail-closed behavior to avoid duplicate messages

## Logging requirements
- Structured JSON logs with: `test_name`, `phase`, `correlation_id`, `duration_ms`.
- For denials: `decision=deny`, `reason_code`, `evidence` (object IDs).
- Never log bot tokens or raw message bodies by default.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI.
- Produces a clear, searchable log trail on failure.
- Integrates cleanly with the shared E2E/compliance framework (no bespoke harness logic that can’t be reused).

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-s5u65","title":"[PERF] fcp-store::MemorySymbolStore::get_symbol: read path takes global write lock","description":"crates/fcp-store/src/symbol_store.rs:392-455. get_symbol(), get_all_symbols(), and symbol_count() all acquire objects.write() just to scrub corrupt symbols and read one object's symbol map. Under concurrent request load that turns every symbol lookup into a serialized writer critical section, blocking unrelated readers and writers on the entire store. The hot-path cost is contention rather than raw CPU: aggregate throughput collapses as lookup concurrency rises even when no corruption is present. Proposed improvement: make the fast path read-only (objects.read()), move corruption scrubbing to a background or per-object maintenance path, or shard/per-object lock the symbol maps so a single lookup does not take the global write lock.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:49:27.974311Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:49:27.974311Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-s5u65","title":"[PERF] fcp-store::MemorySymbolStore::get_symbol: read path takes global write lock","description":"crates/fcp-store/src/symbol_store.rs:392-455. get_symbol(), get_all_symbols(), and symbol_count() all acquire objects.write() just to scrub corrupt symbols and read one object's symbol map. Under concurrent request load that turns every symbol lookup into a serialized writer critical section, blocking unrelated readers and writers on the entire store. The hot-path cost is contention rather than raw CPU: aggregate throughput collapses as lookup concurrency rises even when no corruption is present. Proposed improvement: make the fast path read-only (objects.read()), move corruption scrubbing to a background or per-object maintenance path, or shard/per-object lock the symbol maps so a single lookup does not take the global write lock.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:49:27.974311Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:11:06.764103Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:11:06.763587Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2836,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-s5u65","author":"jemanuel","text":"Resolved in commit 24745bb0.

Fix: read-first + write-on-corruption across all three read methods:
- get_symbol: objects.read() fast path for the healthy case; falls through to objects.write() ONLY when a mismatched symbol is found at the target ESI. Hot case never takes the write lock.
- get_all_symbols / symbol_count: pure read-lock paths that filter via symbol_matches_meta at read time instead of physically scrubbing the map. Observable output unchanged.

Physical pruning of corrupt entries defers to the next write op (put_symbol, put_object_meta, delete_object) which already hold the write lock. used_bytes may lag briefly on corrupt entries — acceptable given it's already eventually-consistent under concurrent puts.

Regression tests:
- get_symbol_read_path_is_concurrent_safe: 32 OS threads × (8 get_symbol + symbol_count) on the same object; asserts every reader agrees count == 8. Under the old code readers serialized via the global write lock.
- get_symbol_fast_path_errors_preserve_semantics: pins ObjectNotFound / NotFound error shapes so the read-lock contract can't silently regress.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-store — 805 pass (803 + 2 new), same 2 pre-existing flaky quarantine::evict_expired* failures.

— cod_4 / SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:11:06Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-s73","title":"fcp.github: GitHub API Connector","description":"# fcp.github - GitHub API Connector (FCP2)

## Strategic Context

### Value Assessment
**Strategic Value Score: 95/100** — Critical Tier 1 connector (Development family).

**Why Critical**: GitHub is **the dominant code hosting platform**. Enables agents to manage repositories, issues, PRs, CI/CD workflows, and code search. Essential for any software development automation.

### Priority Matrix Position
- **Category**: Critical Infrastructure (Development + Automation)
- **Build Phase**: Phase 1 Foundation (immediate)

---

## V2 Specification Compliance (NORMATIVE)

### ConnectorArchetype
```rust
archetypes: vec\![
    ConnectorArchetype::Operational,  // REST/GraphQL actions
    ConnectorArchetype::Streaming,    // Webhook event ingestion
]
```
- **Operational**: CRUD on repos, issues, PRs, workflows
- **Streaming**: Webhook events (push, PR, issue updates)

### ConnectorStateModel
```rust
state_model: ConnectorStateModel::SingletonWriter
```
- **SingletonWriter**: Cursor/sync state for webhook event processing
- State tracked: `{ last_event_ids: HashMap<RepoId, Vec<EventId>> }`
- Lease required for writer fencing

### ConnectorFormat
```rust
format: ConnectorFormat::Wasi
```
- **WASI**: Contains Dangerous operations (repo delete, workflow dispatch)
- Memory isolation for credential handling
- Capability-gated hostcalls

### NetworkConstraints (NORMATIVE)
```rust
NetworkConstraints {
    // GitHub.com API
    host_allow: vec\!["api.github.com".into()],
    // GitHub Enterprise: configured hostname added dynamically
    port_allow: vec\![443],
    deny_localhost: true,
    deny_private_ranges: true,
    deny_tailnet_ranges: true,
    deny_ip_literals: true,
    require_sni: true,
    require_host_canonicalization: true,
}
```

### TaintFlags (NORMATIVE)
```rust
// User inputs (issue bodies, PR descriptions) are tainted
user_content.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::USER_SUPPLIED
    | TaintFlags::PROMPT_SURFACE;

// Webhook payloads are external
webhook_event.provenance.taint = TaintFlags::EXTERNAL_INPUT;

// URLs in content are unverified
if content.contains_urls() {
    content.provenance.taint |= TaintFlags::UNVERIFIED_LINK;
}
```

### SafetyTier per Operation
| Operation | SafetyTier | Rationale |
|-----------|------------|-----------|
| `github.repos.read` | Safe | Read-only listing |
| `github.issues.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `github.pulls.read` | Safe | Read-only |
| `github.search.read` | Safe | Code/issue search |
| `github.issues.write` | Risky | Creates/modifies issues |
| `github.pulls.write` | Risky | Creates/modifies PRs |
| `github.repos.write` | Risky | Modifies repo settings |
| `github.actions.dispatch` | Dangerous | Triggers CI/CD workflows |
| `github.webhooks.manage` | Dangerous | Modifies event routing |
| `github.repos.delete` | Dangerous | Irreversible deletion |

---

## Agent Capabilities (Concrete Use Cases)

### Repository Operations
- List/create/update repositories
- Manage branches and tags
- Access file contents
- Configure settings

### Issue Management
- Create and update issues
- Add comments and labels
- Assign users
- Link to PRs

### Pull Request Workflows
- Create and review PRs
- Request/submit reviews
- Merge strategies
- Check status

### CI/CD Integration
- List workflow runs
- Dispatch workflows
- Cancel/rerun jobs
- Access logs

### Code Search
- Search code across repos
- Search issues and PRs
- Advanced query syntax

### Examples of Agent Workflows
```
"Create an issue for this bug"
"Open a PR with these changes"
"Merge the PR after checks pass"
"Search for usages of this function"
"Trigger the deployment workflow"
"List all open PRs for review"
"Add labels to issues matching criteria"
```

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### Linear Integration
**Issue bidirectional sync:**
- GitHub issues ↔ Linear issues
- PR references in Linear
- Coordinated status updates

### Agent Mail Integration
- PR review requests → agent threads
- Issue assignments trigger workflows
- CI/CD notifications routed

### Sentry Integration
- Error events create GitHub issues
- Stack traces linked to code
- Release tracking

### Datadog/Grafana Integration
- Deployment events correlated
- Performance metrics linked to commits
- Incident → PR correlation

---

## Technical Implementation Notes

### API Surface
- **REST API v3**: Primary CRUD operations
- **GraphQL API v4**: Efficient queries, mutations
- **Webhooks**: Event delivery

### Recommended Crates
- `octocrab` — GitHub API client
- `reqwest` — HTTP client
- `graphql_client` — GraphQL queries
- `hmac` / `sha2` — Webhook signature validation

### Authentication Modes
| Mode | Use Case | Recommendation |
|------|----------|----------------|
| GitHub App | Organization automation | **Preferred** |
| OAuth App | User-delegated access | Good |
| PAT | Personal use | Discouraged |

---

## Zone Architecture

### Recommended Zones
| Zone | Description | Capabilities |
|------|-------------|--------------|
| `z:work:github:<org>` | Organization-scoped | Full org access |
| `z:project:<name>:github` | Repo-scoped | Single repo |
| `z:work:github:readonly` | Read-only | Safe operations only |

### Security Considerations
- Org-level access is high trust
- Repo-scoped tokens preferred
- Webhook secrets per installation

---

## Manifest Archetypes (Closed Set)
- `operational` — REST/GraphQL actions
- `streaming` — Webhook event ingestion

---

## External Surface

### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only:
- `api.github.com:443` (GitHub.com)
- Enterprise: configured hostname

### Credential Injection
- GitHub App private key + installation token
- OAuth tokens via egress proxy
- Webhook secrets for signature validation

---

## Capability Model

### Read Operations
- `github.repos.read` — List/get repositories
- `github.issues.read` — List/get issues
- `github.pulls.read` — List/get PRs
- `github.actions.read` — Workflow/run status
- `github.search.read` — Code/issue search

### Write Operations
- `github.repos.write` — Update repo settings
- `github.issues.write` — Create/update issues
- `github.pulls.write` — Create/update PRs
- `github.actions.write` — Cancel/rerun jobs

### Admin Operations
- `github.repos.delete` — Delete repository (Dangerous)
- `github.actions.dispatch` — Trigger workflows (Dangerous)
- `github.webhooks.manage` — Configure webhooks (Dangerous)

---

## Operations (Representative)

### List Repositories
```
GET /orgs/{org}/repos
GET /user/repos
```

### Create Issue
```json
POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues
{
  "title": "Bug: login fails",
  "body": "Steps to reproduce...",
  "labels": ["bug", "priority:high"]
}
```

### Create Pull Request
```json
POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls
{
  "title": "Fix login bug",
  "head": "fix-login",
  "base": "main",
  "body": "Fixes #123"
}
```

### Dispatch Workflow
```json
POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/workflows/{workflow_id}/dispatches
{
  "ref": "main",
  "inputs": {"environment": "staging"}
}
```

### Search Code
```
GET /search/code?q=function+repo:owner/repo
```

---

## Tests

### Unit Tests (Mock-Only)
- API request construction
- Response parsing
- Webhook signature validation
- Rate limit handling
- Error responses

### Integration Tests
- Mock GitHub API responses
- Multi-step workflows (create PR → review → merge)
- Webhook event processing

### E2E Compliance
- Validates NetworkConstraints (api.github.com only)
- Dangerous operation approval flow
- Audit logging for writes

---

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner (static + dynamic)
- No real GitHub API calls in unit tests
- All CRUD operations functional
- Webhook signature validation works
- Rate limiting respected
- GraphQL queries supported
- All V2 NORMATIVE requirements satisfied","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:55:27.085625247Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-02T01:38:43.362078Z","closed_at":"2026-03-02T01:38:43.362059Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-s73","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.18","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-s73","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-s73","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-s73","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.21.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-s73","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.26","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-s73","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.31","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-s73","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-iv7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-s73","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-k4ka","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-s73","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-krt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-s73.1","title":"fcp.github: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)","description":"# fcp.github: Manifest + Capability Map (FCP2)

## Goal
Define the GitHub connector manifest contract:
- archetypes (`operational`, `streaming`)
- operations + schema IDs
- capability families + per-operation caps
- NetworkConstraints allowlist for GitHub API host(s)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Complete manifest spec stored here.
- Good/bad manifest examples exist for static compliance.
